title: Inserting two symbols with same name
body:
---


Author Name: **J Baxter**
Original Redmine Issue: 83 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2009-05-06
Original Assignee: Wilson Snyder (@wsnyder)

---

Hi,

I've got a netlist from a synthesis tool, and I think it's confusing Verilator.

This is the output I'm seeing from a little test case I've put together, but it's doing the exact same thing in a larger design:

```
verilator -language 1364-2001 -Wno-lint --top-module intercon -sc intercon.vm -v ./sim_lib.v
%Error: intercon.vm:1802: Internal Error: ../V3SymTable.h:53: Inserting two symbols with same name: v__DOT__wb4m1s2__DOT__N_15
%Error: Command Failed /home/orsoc1/Documents/julius/scratch/verilator-3.702/verilator_bin -language 1364-2001 -Wno-lint --top-module intercon -sc intercon.vm -v ./sim_lib.v
make: *** [all] Error 10

```

The signal in question, wbm1s2.N_15, is declared as a wire, like so:
wire \wb4m1s2.N_15  ;

And is used to hook up two modules inside the intercon module.

Attached is a test case.
